name: Multi-Source BFS
summary: Start the search from EVERY zero at once, all at distance 0. A single breadth-first wave then stamps each cell with the moment it is first reached — which is its distance to the nearest zero.
visualizationIntent: init: build the dist grid (all -1) and an empty queue. update: seed the queue with every zero cell at distance 0. loop: serve the oldest cell in the queue. move: examine its four neighbours; each unreached neighbour is stamped with this cell's distance + 1 and enqueued. return: show the completed distance grid.

lineExplanations.3: Record how many rows the matrix has.
lineExplanations.4: Record how many columns each row has.
lineExplanations.6: Loop over each row to build a matching distance row.
lineExplanations.7: Fill the row with -1 — our marker for 'not reached yet'.
lineExplanations.9: Loop over each row to build a matching state row.
lineExplanations.10: Fill it with zeros — every cell starts unreached.
lineExplanations.11: Placeholder for the current cell's row; -1 means 'none yet'.
lineExplanations.12: Placeholder for the current cell's column.
lineExplanations.13: The four orthogonal moves — down, up, right, left.
lineExplanations.15: Scan every row looking for zeros.
lineExplanations.16: Scan every column of this row.
lineExplanations.17: Is this cell a zero — a source of the search?
lineExplanations.18: A zero is distance 0 from itself.
lineExplanations.19: Mark it discovered so it is never queued twice.
lineExplanations.20: Seed the queue with this zero — all zeros start the wave together.
lineExplanations.21: Process the queue until every cell has been reached.
lineExplanations.22: Take the cell from the FRONT — the oldest discovered, so distances come out in increasing order.
lineExplanations.26: Try each of the four directions.
lineExplanations.29: Is the neighbour in-bounds and still unreached?
lineExplanations.30: Its distance is this cell's distance plus one — one step farther from the nearest zero.
lineExplanations.32: Add it to the back of the queue to continue the wave.
lineExplanations.33: Every cell now holds its distance to the nearest zero — return the grid.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: is the class wrapper LeetCode expects.
syntaxExplanations.2: def updateMatrix(self, mat): mat is the binary matrix of 0s and 1s.
syntaxExplanations.4: mat[0] is the first row; len(...) gives the column count.
syntaxExplanations.5: [] creates an empty list to hold distance rows.
syntaxExplanations.6: for r in range(rows): loops r over each row index.
syntaxExplanations.7: [-1] * cols builds a row of that many -1s; .append adds it.
syntaxExplanations.9: for r in range(rows): loops again to size the state grid.
syntaxExplanations.10: [0] * cols builds a row of zeros; .append adds it.
syntaxExplanations.11: cur_r = -1 sets a sentinel; -1 is not a valid index.
syntaxExplanations.12: cur_c = -1 does the same for the column.
syntaxExplanations.13: A list of [row-delta, col-delta] pairs for the 4 directions.
syntaxExplanations.14: [] makes an empty list used as a queue.
syntaxExplanations.15: for r in range(rows): scans every row for zeros.
syntaxExplanations.16: for c in range(cols): scans every column.
syntaxExplanations.17: mat[r][c] == 0 tests whether this cell is a source.
syntaxExplanations.18: dist[r][c] = 0 records a zero's own distance.
syntaxExplanations.20: append([r, c]) seeds the queue with this source.
syntaxExplanations.21: while len(queue) > 0: runs until the queue drains.
syntaxExplanations.22: queue.pop(0) removes and returns the FRONT item — index 0.
syntaxExplanations.23: cell[0] reads the pair's first element (row).
syntaxExplanations.25: state[cur_r][cur_c] = 2 marks the cell fully processed.
syntaxExplanations.26: for d in dirs: loops over each direction.
syntaxExplanations.27: cur_r + d[0] gives the neighbour's row.
syntaxExplanations.28: cur_c + d[1] gives the neighbour's column.
syntaxExplanations.29: Chained 'and' checks bounds and that the neighbour is still unreached (state 0).
syntaxExplanations.30: dist[cur_r][cur_c] + 1 is one step farther than the current cell.
syntaxExplanations.33: return dist hands back the finished distance grid.
